name: atelier-hb-auth-module
description: HB Auth — the credentials & login-methods manager for agents: an account registry / recipe book over the vault, LastPass + SMS methods (convention path), and the ENFORCED Bitwarden broker — a signed local daemon that holds the only vault session and gates every credential behind per-credential policy, an origin check, and a native macOS approval. Secrets never enter the model's context. macOS only.
author: pa1nd

atelier.note: macOS only. ENFORCED path (Bitwarden broker): needs the Bitwarden CLI (bw) + Swift / Xcode Command Line Tools to compile the signed broker daemon on first run. Run `hb-broker setup` once (shown on the Bitwarden source page): you enter your master password on stdin (never the browser), it mints a long-lived BW_SESSION token, stores ONLY that token in the login Keychain bound to the daemon's own code signature, and gates every use behind per-credential policy, an origin check read from the browser, and a native macOS approval prompt — in practice your login password, since a background LaunchAgent can't present the Touch ID sheet. The daemon installs to ~/Library/Application Support/hb-broker and runs as a per-user LaunchAgent. Convention path (optional): the LastPass CLI (lpass) + a dedicated agent account for the older LastPass/SMS recipes; SMS 2FA needs SMSPOOL_API_KEY in the instance .env. Drives any Chrome exposing CDP on 127.0.0.1:9223 (horse-browser is the usual one; override with HB_CDP_PORT), detected but installed separately. No secret lives in the module folder — passwords/TOTP stay in the vault; the session token lives only in the Keychain + broker memory.
